info.title: OpertionsPizza API
info.description: API documentation for the OpertionsPizza scheduling and employee management system.

sanctumAuth.description: Laravel Sanctum Bearer Token. Obtain via /login endpoint.

Unauthenticated.description: Unauthenticated - Bearer token missing or invalid

tags.name: Employee Skills
tags.description: Assign and manage skills per employee per store

post.description: Authenticate a user and receive a Bearer token. No authentication required.

email.description: Must be a valid email format. Must exist in users table.

post.description: Revoke the current user's Bearer token.

get.description: Returns all employees with their assigned skills and ratings. Note: the SkillService getAll() method returns employees grouped with their skills, not a plain skill list.

post.description: Creates a new skill. Name is normalized (trimmed, lowercase then ucwords, extra spaces collapsed). Duplicate names (case/space insensitive) are rejected.

name.description: Required. Max 255 chars. Must be unique (case & space insensitive). Will be normalized to Title Case.

description.description: Optional. Will be normalized to Title Case.

get.description: Returns skills that have been soft deleted, ordered by ID ascending.

put.description: Updates a skill by ID. All fields are optional (partial update supported). Name uniqueness check excludes current skill.

name.description: Optional. Must be unique (case & space insensitive, excluding self). Will be normalized.

delete.description: Soft deletes a skill. If the skill is assigned to employees, a 409 conflict is returned.

409.description: Skill is in use and cannot be deleted

message.example: Cannot delete this skill because it is assigned to employees.

post.description: Restores a previously soft-deleted skill. Returns 409 if the skill is not currently deleted.

get.summary: List employee skills for a store
get.description: Returns all employee-skill assignments for employees belonging to the given store, grouped by employee.

post.summary: Assign a skill to an employee
post.description: Creates a new employee-skill record. The employee must belong to the store. Duplicate skill per employee is rejected.

employee_id.description: Required. Employee must exist and belong to this store.

skill_id.description: Required. Must exist in skills table. Must be unique per employee.

put.description: Partially updates an employee skill record. All fields are optional. Uniqueness check for skill_id excludes the current record.

employee_id.description: Optional. Must belong to the store.

skill_id.description: Optional. Must be unique per employee (excluding current record).

delete.description: Permanently deletes the employee-skill assignment record.

post.description: Submits one or more day-off requests for employees. No authentication required. Duplicate requests for the same employee+date (both in DB and within the same batch) are rejected.

requests.description: Array of day-off requests. No duplicate employee+date within batch.

employee_id.description: Required. Must exist in employees table.

date.description: Required. Must be a valid date. Employee must not already have a request on this date.

note.description: Required. String note for the request.

get.summary: List all day-off requests for a store
get.description: Returns all day-off records for employees belonging to the given store. Ordered by employee_id then date ascending.

put.description: Updates a day-off record. Used by managers to approve/reject and add notes. The accepted_by field is automatically set to the authenticated user's ID when acceptedStatus is changed.

date.description: Optional. Must not create duplicate for same employee.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a day-off record belonging to the store.

get.summary: List employee availabilities for a store
get.description: Returns availabilities for all employees in the store, grouped by employee, ordered by employee_id then day of week (Mon-Sun).

post.description: Creates availability for an employee on a specific day with time ranges. Validates: employee belongs to store, day not already set for this employee, times do not overlap (within batch and with existing DB records), from < to.

employee_id.description: Required. Employee must exist and belong to this store.

day_of_week.description: Required. Case insensitive (normalized to lowercase). Must be unique per employee.

times.description: Required. At least one time range. Times must not overlap each other or existing records.

from.description: Required. Format H:i (24hr). Must be less than 'to'.

to.description: Required. Format H:i (24hr). Must be greater than 'from'.

put.description: Updates an existing availability record. Times are replaced entirely (old times deleted, new ones inserted). Validates overlap against other availability records.

employee_id.description: Optional. Must belong to the store.

day_of_week.description: Optional. Must be unique per employee (excluding current record).

times.description: Required. Replaces all existing times. Must not overlap.

delete.description: Deletes an availability record and all its associated time ranges.

post.description: Returns all employees belonging to a specific store.

store_id.description: Required. Must exist in stores table.

get.description: Returns paginated master schedules for the store, ordered by store_id then start_date. Max per_page is 50.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of records per page. Max 50.

get.description: Returns all published master schedules for the store, ordered by start_date ascending.
Returns only soft-deleted master schedules for the store with their schedules.

post.summary: Create a master schedule week for a store

get.summary: Get a master schedule by ID
get.description: Returns a specific master schedule with its child schedules.

delete.description: Soft deletes the master schedule and all its child schedule records in a single transaction.

put.summary: Update a master schedule with schedules
put.description: Updates a master schedule. Schedules can be added, updated, or removed. Send existing schedule IDs to update them; omit IDs for new schedules; schedules not included in the payload are deleted. Actual times can be recorded here. Validates the same business rules as create.

start_date.description: Optional. Must be a Tuesday if provided.

end_date.description: Optional. Must be a Monday if provided. Must be exactly 7 days after start_date.

schedules.description: Optional. If provided, replaces all schedules (by ID matching). Schedules without an ID are created.

id.description: Optional. If provided and exists, updates that record. If omitted, creates new.

actual_start_time.description: Optional. Actual clock-in time. Format H:i.

actual_end_time.description: Optional. Actual clock-out time. Must be after actual_start_time if both provided.

post.description: Marks a master schedule as published. Validation: schedule must not already be published, must have at least one schedule record, and must have at least one schedule for every date in the range.

422.description: Already published or missing schedule dates

published: This master schedule is already published.

post.description: Marks a master schedule as unpublished. Returns 422 if already unpublished.

published: This master schedule is already unpublished.

post.description: Restores a soft-deleted master schedule and all its soft-deleted child schedules. Returns 422 if the record is not deleted.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a master schedule and all its child schedules. The record MUST be soft-deleted first. Returns 422 if not in trash.

force_delete: You must delete the record first before force deleting.

delete.summary: Soft delete a single schedule record
delete.description: Soft deletes a specific schedule (child record). Cannot delete if: the parent master schedule is published, or if the schedule has actual_start_time or actual_end_time recorded.

422.description: Cannot delete (published schedule or has actual times)

schedule: Cannot delete schedule from a published master schedule.

post.description: Restores a specific soft-deleted child schedule. Returns 422 if the schedule is not currently deleted.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a specific child schedule. Must be soft-deleted first.

force_delete: You must delete the schedule first before force deleting.

get.description: Returns all data needed to build a schedule for a date range: the latest published schedule overlapping the range, approved days off, employees with their availability and skills, and all available skills. Optionally filter by a single employee.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Required. Start of the scheduling window.

parameters.name: employee_id
parameters.description: Optional. Filter results to a single employee. Must belong to the store.

published_schedule.description: The most recent published master schedule overlapping the requested date range.

days_off.description: Approved day-off records for store employees within the date range.

employees.description: All employees in the store with their availability and skills loaded.

post.description: Creates a new master schedule by copying shifts from a source. If master_schedule_id is provided, copies from that specific schedule. Otherwise copies from the previous week. Dates are offset to match the new week. Validation: start_date must be Tuesday, end_date must be Monday, must be exactly 7 days, schedule must not already exist for those dates, and only the next sequential week is allowed (based on latest existing schedule).

start_date.description: Required. Must be a Tuesday. Must be the next sequential week.

end_date.description: Required. Must be a Monday. Must be exactly 7 days after start_date.

master_schedule_id.description: Optional. ID of the source master schedule to copy from. If omitted, copies from previous week.

422.description: Validation error (date rules, overlap, no previous week, etc.)

get.description: Returns paginated schedule templates associated with the given store, ordered by ID ascending. Max per_page is 50.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of records per page. Max 50.

post.description: Creates a new schedule template. Two modes: (1) General template — provide 'details' array with day/time/skill entries. (2) Export from master schedule — provide 'master_schedule_id' to auto-generate template details from that schedule's shifts (days are inferred from shift dates).

master_schedule_id.description: Optional. If provided, template details are imported from this master schedule. Mutually exclusive with 'details'.

details.description: Required if master_schedule_id is not provided. Array of template shift details.

end_time.description: Required. Format H:i. Must be after start_time.

skill_id.description: Required. Must exist in skills table.

post.summary: Save a template from a master schedule
post.description: Same as POST /schedule-templates/save but with a master_schedule_id in the URL path. Creates a template from the given master schedule's shifts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the master schedule to export as a template

master_schedule_id.description: Required when using this route. Must exist in master_schedule table.

post.description: Previews what a template would look like applied to a specific week. Returns calculated shift dates (starting from start_date) based on the template's day_of_week entries. Day mapping: tuesday=+0, wednesday=+1, thursday=+2, friday=+3, saturday=+4, sunday=+5, monday=+6.

template_id.description: Required. Must exist in schedule_templates table.

start_date.description: Required. Must be a Tuesday (week start).

get.description: Returns a specific schedule template with its detail records.
